reason: retrofit:SingleAdminCollaborator / retrofit:CrossCuttingSingleAdmin (../load-path.ttl): AP-001's required-review branch protection cannot be meaningfully satisfied with only one collaborator on the repository -- there is no second reviewer to approve a PR the sole admin didn't author. AP-001 explicitly declines to change collaborator grants; this packet names the actual gap.
design_note: WHO the second admin/collaborator should be, and at what permission level, is a real external trust decision that cannot be derived from repository law or evidence -- this is the DESIGN_UNCERTAINTY_LAW escalation exception, not an internal design choice being deferred out of caution.
plan_hash_blocked_reason: Cannot be computed until the user supplies the target account identity and permission level -- both are required inputs to the exact API payload.
creates: a collaborator invitation on seanchatmangpt/ggen at the specified permission level (pending until accepted)
post_checks: a test PR authored by the prior sole admin can be approved by the new collaborator, confirming AP-001's required-review rule is now satisfiable
explicitly_out_of_scope: ReleasePath tag-push/workflow_dispatch protection on release.yml -- a separate GitHub settings surface, needs its own packet, not yet authored
FleetManagementPath cloud-actuation identity -- tracked separately as retrofit:FleetCloudIdentityUnverified, genuinely Unknown, no cloud IAM access exists in this environment
invalidation_condition: Regenerate if seanchatmangpt/ggen's collaborator list changes before this executes, or if the user's intended second-admin identity changes.
required_authorization: explicit user ! command supplying the target account identity and permission level, then executing the operation above -- this packet does not authorize itself and cannot even be fully planned without user input
